body.title: Wire Librarian research completion to intent extractor validate()
body.result: Librarian research completion wired to intent extractor validation — _seed_intent_extractor() calls validate(prediction_id=None) after successful research with non-empty answer; fails open on device unavailability; 3 tests verify behavior (call made, fail-open on ImportError, fail-open on Exception)
body.intention: I intend that Librarian research completions generate validate(prediction_id=None) signals to the intent extractor, seeding the learning store with research-domain ground truth without any pre-hoc prediction.
body.description:
Librarian.research() produces genuine unknowns. Post-research, the query intent and what was actually useful is a clean ground-truth signal with no prediction noise.

After research() completes and deposits nodes: call intent_extractor.validate(prediction_id=None, actual_outcome=summary_of_useful_findings, domain=research). This seeds the intent extractor with research-domain examples.

Never call predict() in the Librarian research path — explicitly excluded by design.

**Affected files:** devices/librarian/ (research completion hook — read devices/librarian/ at sprint time to find correct hook point)
**Design rules:** external-state; post-hoc only; no SQLite; fail-open if intent device unavailable
**Scope boundary:** IN — validate() call at research completion. OUT — predict() in Librarian path (excluded); changing what Librarian deposits.
**Completion criteria:** After Librarian research() call, devlab.validations contains new row with prediction_id=NULL; existing Librarian tests pass; new test verifies validate() called on completion; hook fails open if intent device is down.
